votes.reason: The Python code implements the exact logic of the Coq specification, handling both cases (enough remaining vs not enough) identically.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq precondition and specification, correctly handling both cases for remaining carrots.
The Python code correctly implements the precondition and specification defined in the Coq code, matching the logic for both cases of remaining carrots.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The precondition checks the same bounds (0 <= number, need, remaining <= 1000). The spec checks the same two cases: if remaining >= need, result should be [number + need, remaining - need]; otherwise result should be [number + remaining, 0]. The Coq uses Z.ge and Z.lt which correspond to >= and < in Python, and the list construction matches exactly.

The Coq uses Z.ge (>=) and Z.lt (<) which correspond exactly to the Python >= and < (via else) comparisons.
